name: same weight re-expressed lb->kg at the same instant raises a false fact_conflict
description: Adversarial unit-change probe. Note 1 records Me.bodyWeight = 180 lb for a given instant; note 2 records the SAME reading at the SAME validity instant re-expressed as 81.6 kg (180 lb ~= 81.6 kg — physically the same measurement, just unit-converted). supersession.values_equal used to compare value_json dicts literally, so {value:180,unit:lb} != {value:81.6,unit:kg} hit the same-instant clash branch and filed a false fact_conflict, parking the second reading in pending_review even though the two are the SAME measurement. GAP (closed): values_equal now normalizes {value, unit} payloads through a deliberately tiny conversion table (lb<->kg, ft/in<->cm, F<->C) with a small relative tolerance for re-rounding, FOR COMPARISON ONLY — values are stored verbatim. Equal-after-normalization at the same instant refreshes in place (no conflict, one active reading); genuinely different values keep the conflict path. SPEC-CORRECT expectation encoded: no fact_conflict, one active reading.

steps.body: Weighed in at 180 lb this morning.
Correcting yesterday's weigh-in to metric: 81.6 kg.
